message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Tasks 5-6 done, proceed with Task 7">
Status update:

**Tasks 5 and 6 are COMPLETE.** All a11y fixes applied, CI green on `6c757bc`.

**Task 7 (View todo list + Add todo) is now the active task.** I see some of you have already started domain types for todos (todo_item.rs, todo_title.rs, etc.) in the working tree. That's the right next task — just make sure you follow the commit discipline:

1. Get to green → commit → push → verify CI
2. Do NOT accumulate uncommitted work
3. One thing at a time

**Commit discipline rules**: We have 8/9 consents (everyone except Carson and Luca). That's well past consensus. **Kent: please add the Commit Discipline section to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md and commit it now.** Carson and Luca can register concerns after the fact if they have any.

**Carson, Luca**: Your consent votes are still welcome but we're proceeding with adoption.

Let's go. Task 7 — view todo list and add a todo item.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- the stop is lifted and Task 7 is active. Let me first handle my queued CSS items (the minor fixes), then move on to Task 7 CSS needs. Let me check the current state of the working tree.

Use :focus-visible instead of :focus on skip link

Prevents the skip link from appearing on mouse click while
still revealing it for keyboard navigation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command: git diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- static/css/main.css | head -60
input.description: Check what actually changed in the commit

content.text:
It looks like another teammate added the `.site-header` and `.button--text` CSS to the file between my reads, and it got included in this commit. That's the concurrent editing problem again. The comment change is correct, but it also picked up the header component CSS that someone else added.

This is actually fine -- the header CSS was needed anyway and it's correct. But I should be transparent about this. Let me now do Fix 3: add the generic `aria-invalid` base rule, then Fix 4: the header CSS is already committed (included in the last commit), so I can skip that.
Let me find the right place for the `aria-invalid` base rule -- after the focus-visible block or near the form input styles.
I'll add the generic `aria-invalid` rule right after the input block closes, before labels.

content.content: The file /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css has been updated successfully.

lines: +  /* Invalid inputs -- danger border for any input with aria-invalid */

content.text: Now let me also remove the component-scoped version since the base rule covers it.

content.content: The file /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css has been updated successfully.

lines: -  /* Error state on inputs -- uses :has() for parent-based styling.
-   * When an input has aria-invalid, the parent group gets a danger border. */

input.command:
git add /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Move aria-invalid border style to base layer

Generic [aria-invalid="true"] rule in the base layer replaces the
component-scoped .auth-form__group:has() version. Applies danger
border to any invalid input, not just auth forms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.content:
CSS fixes committed and pushed. Three separate commits per discipline rules:

1. **`dd038a1`** - Use `:focus-visible` instead of `:focus` on skip link
2. **`7979586`** - Add contrast usage restriction comment to `--color-success` (note: this commit also picked up `.site-header` and `.button--text` CSS that another teammate added to the file concurrently)
3. **`22662be`** - Move `aria-invalid` border style to base layer (generic rule replaces auth-form-scoped version)

All pushed to origin. I also unstaged Task 7 files that other teammates had inadvertently staged.

I'm now ready to contribute CSS for Task 7 (todo list page). What does the team need from me? I can work on:
- Todo list component styles (list layout, item display, completion states)
- Add-todo form styling
- Empty state styling

Waiting for direction on what templates/class names are being used.
